preamble: The transformation of a voiced velar stop [g] into a voiced retroflex affricate [ɖʐ]. This voiced counterpart to the [k] -> [ʈʂ] shift involves palatalization followed by hardening and retraction.

examples.note: Parallel to the voiceless shift, Proto-Slavic *g became a soft affricate which eventually hardened into the retroflex [ɖʐ] (written 'dż').

sources: Swan, O. E. (2002). A Grammar of Contemporary Polish.
Shevelov, G. Y. (1964). A Prehistory of Slavic.
